$comment: SS6 source of truth (saas-secrets-sync): per-integration documents in AWS Secrets Manager, each holding the non-secret config AND the secret(s) for ONE provider integration. Non-integration secrets (repo/account-generated, not tied to a provider) live in a single platform-secrets doc. assemble.mjs projects this into (a) the per-worker secret view committed as secrets.manifest.json and consumed by check.mjs/sync.mjs, and (b) the per-worker config view rendered into wrangler vars. config keys are non-secret and may be logged; secret keys never are.

github-app.$comment: GITHUB_APP_ID/SLUG/CLIENT_ID are not sensitive, but the deploy lane only pushes `secret`-classified keys to the worker (non-secret `config` is hand-maintained in each worker's wrangler vars). integrations-worker reads GITHUB_APP_ID/SLUG at runtime via getConfiguredProvider, so all App keys are classified as secrets to flow through secrets-live — matching the worker template's documented expectation.

slack-app.$comment: Per-environment Slack App credentials (IH1 connect flow + IH3 signed-request verification), escrow-managed alongside the GitHub App's. ACTIVE: the Slack App is registered per environment and integrations/slack-app/<env> is seeded, so secrets-live requires + delivers these to integrations-worker.

supabase-oauth.$comment: Per-environment Supabase OAuth app credentials (IH6 connect + token refresh), escrow-managed alongside the GitHub/Slack apps. ACTIVE: the Supabase OAuth app is registered per environment and integrations/supabase-oauth/<env> is seeded, so secrets-live requires + delivers these to integrations-worker.

cloudflare-oauth.$comment: Per-environment Cloudflare OAuth client credentials (IH5, risks D3 resolved: Cloudflare shipped OAuth clients for the API), escrow-managed alongside the GitHub/Slack/Supabase apps. ACTIVE: the console presents Cloudflare as an OAuth connect, so the OAuth client is registered per environment and integrations/cloudflare-oauth/<env> is seeded; secrets-live requires + delivers these to integrations-worker. When absent the adapter still falls back to the token-paste posture. CLOUDFLARE_OAUTH_SCOPE is optionalConfig: the whitespace-separated scope list requested at consent (must be a subset of the client's registered scopes; the adapter always appends offline_access). Unset → the adapter's account:read+user:read default, enough for consent + refresh but not child-token minting — set it to the client's real scope names (including account 'API Tokens Write') to enable minting.
